steps.title: Recognize the perfect square on the right side
steps.content: We have the equation: $$x^2 = 40 \times 40$$ The right side is 40 multiplied by itself, which we can write as $40^2$. Therefore: $$x^2 = 40^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the positive value of x
steps.content: Since $x^2 = 40^2$ and we need the positive solution, take the positive square root of both sides: $$x = 40$$
